title: The Shipping Impact of Simandou's Commissioning
section_path: The Shipping Impact of Simandou's Commissioning
section_path_text: The Shipping Impact of Simandou's Commissioning
summary:
Main
Image reference: assets/2025-11-28_the-shipping-impact-of-simandous-commissioning_img_rgb-color-brs-shipbrokers-monogram-l_c365f35f1de5.png
In mid-November, the Guinean Port of Morebaya held the commissioning ceremony for the Simandou iron ore project, marking the mine's official launch. Baowu Group reported that the $20 billion project integrates mining, rail, and port facilities. In mid-October, the first batches of ore were transported via the Trans-Guinea Railway to the country's western coast and delivered to Morebaya, the dedicated export terminal for Simandou.
